summary: This is a brief announcement post directing readers to Cathy O'Neil's Bloomberg View column about the myth of genius in mathematics. The post itself contains minimal content beyond the link and title. The referenced column appears to argue that mathematicians are not all geniuses, challenging common perceptions about mathematical talent. The post serves primarily as a pointer to her external writing rather than containing substantive analysis itself.
key_points: The post announces a Bloomberg View column about genius myths in mathematics
O'Neil challenges the perception that all mathematicians are geniuses
The post is primarily a link aggregator for her Bloomberg writing
money_quotes: A Mathematician's Secret: We're Not All Geniuses
key_insight: The myth that mathematicians must be geniuses is harmful and inaccurate, suggesting mathematical ability is more accessible than commonly believed.
